title: AEP v1.1 F13 Claim Runtime Falsifier
description: v1.1 F13 frontier-break primitive (SPECULATIVE FRONTIER). Four-agent convergence (adversary+judge+forge+curator). Every claim carries an executable falsifier handle that a fresh validator can run in bounded time. Closes the dormitive-virtue NP-2 class structurally at write-time, not via human archaeology. Composes_with: v0.8 F5 self_falsifying (packet-level; F13 is CLAIM-level), v0.8 F2 reproducibility_certificate, sec02 truth-tags, sec50 EH Law-3.

bound_to_claim_id.description: Claim ID this falsifier binds. The claim being falsified.

executor.description: Execution model. python_static_dotted + node_static_dotted are the safe defaults (no shell, no fs writes outside packet_root). subprocess_sandboxed is OPT-IN per FALSIFIER-V80-2 (v0.8 F5 inheritance).

cmd.description: Command in the executor's idiom. For python_static_dotted: 'module.func' resolving to func(claim, packet_root) -> int. For grep_pattern: a regex applied to a designated path. For json_path_assertion: a JSONPath expression with expected-value side.

expected_exit.description: Exit code semantics PER F5 inheritance: 0 = falsifier PASSES (claim survives); non-zero = falsifier FIRES (claim is FALSIFIED). Default 0.

ttl_ms.description: Wall-time bound in ms. Operator constraint: <=100ms per F13 spec. Validator SIGKILLs on overrun and emits AEP11_F13_TIMEOUT.

binding_principal.description: did:key or substrate id of the authority that bound this falsifier. Composes with sec50 Law-3 (anti-self-attestation: binding_principal !== claim author principal).

on_fire_action.description: Action when falsifier fires. REJECT = packet validation FAIL. DEMOTE_RELIABILITY = truth-tag downgraded one tier (R->S->P->E->A->U). WARN = informational only. QUARANTINE = claim isolated in validation results but packet still PASSES.

last_run_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC when falsifier last executed; null if never run.

last_run_exit_code.description: Last observed exit code; null if never run.

f9_quorum_required.description: If true, falsifier must produce same exit across N>=2 substrates per v1.0.x F9. Closes dormitive-virtue + executor-drift class simultaneously.

trivially_true_blocker.description: If true, validator rejects falsifiers whose cmd matches a tautology pattern (e.g., 'exit 0', 'return True').

tautology_patterns_checked.description: List of tautology patterns the validator scanned against.

tautology_check.description: Anti-dormitive-self-binding: a falsifier that always returns expected_exit is tautological and provides no falsification value.
